_comment: Canonical shape contract for the committed developer-dashboard feed `dashboard/data/dashboard.json` — the pinned-feed-contract pattern proven on `botsite/data/console_data_contract.json` (PR #1884), applied to the feed the websites repo's dashboard renders ~12 pages from (menno420/websites dashboard/data_source.py, fetching the committed file over raw.githubusercontent.com). SLICE SEMANTICS, unlike the console contract's total whitelist: only the families listed in `contracted_families` are pinned; every other top-level family stays un-contracted (free to change) until it is deliberately added here, family by family, with a `version` bump — that growth path is the idea's design (docs/ideas/pinned-feed-contract-for-dashboard-json-2026-07-09.md). For a contracted family, the keys listed are the GUARANTEED fields per record (values may be null); a contracted field or family may only be added/renamed/removed by editing THIS file and bumping `version` — the explicit, reviewable act consumers pin against (dashboard.json carries the version as meta.schema_version). The producer (scripts/export_dashboard_data.py DASHBOARD_* constants) and the checker (scripts/check_dashboard_data.py check_dashboard_contract, CI-enforced via tests/unit/scripts/) both validate against this file, so a producer-side rename of a contracted surface fails CI here instead of silently blanking a consumer page (the BUG-0022 desync class). First slice: `meta` (the version-carrying envelope every consumer reads) + `bugs` (websites' bugs page; producer-constructed fixed-key records).
